free_text: Direct capture in the 24Mg(p,g)25Al reaction
45 cm a and 56 cm a Ge(Li) detectors both with
an energy resolution of 2.0 keV at Eg = 1.33 MeV. The
relative efficiency curves for these detectors were
obtained from a 56Co source.
The targets were fabricated by reducing MgO powder
enriched to >= 99.9% in 24Mg. The MgO powder was
first dissolved in triply distilled water, then
painted on a Ta sheet and finally dried. In the
subsequent resistance heating of this Ta sheet in
high vacuum (~ 4 x 10-6 torr) , the Ta backing
oxidized and released, therefore, the 24Mg component.
A second 0.25 mm thick Ta sheet, placed 15 cm from the
evaporating source, was thus coated with 24Mg.
OG
The gamma-ray detector was placed in close
geometry to the target (at angle = 45 degrees,
distance to the target ~ 1 cm).
The isotropic angular distribution of this gamma-ray
was assumed by authors. The authors assumed, for the
452 ---> 0 keV secondary gamma-ray transition, its
intensity represents ~ 85% of the non-resonant
gamma-ray yield through direct feed as well as
gamma-gamma cascades from higher states.
No information on source of uncertainty.
Fig.3 (p.523) in Nucl.Phys.A,242,519,1975 was
digitized by GSYS2.4.7.
The two Ge(Li) detectors were placed at 0
degree and 90 degrees, distance to the target d=3.2 cm.
No information on source of uncertainty.
Fig.4 (p.524)in Nucl.Phys.A,242,519,1975 was
digitized by GSYS2.4.7.
No information on source of uncertainty.

digitized by GSYS2.4.7.
